name: El Romeral and Lillo Lagoons Loop
name_original: Lagunas de la Albardiosa y del Longar – circular desde El Romeral
description: Cycle a moderate 26.0-mile mountain biking route through the unique salt pans and bird-rich lagoons near El Romeral.
region_intro: Mountain bike trails around El Romeral Spain offer diverse landscapes for outdoor activities. The region, particularly in Castilla-La Mancha, features a mix of natural terrain, unpaved roads, and some paved sections. Riders can explore areas characterized by iconic windmills perched on hills and unique salt pans and lagoons, providing varied scenery for mountain biking. The terrain includes gentle ascents and descents, suitable for different skill levels. Best mountain bike trails in El Romeral The most popular mountain bike trail is Tembleque and El Romeral Windmills Trail, a 25.9 miles (41.7 km) trail that takes 3 hours 21 minutes to complete. This route features the iconic windmills of El Romeral. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is El Romeral and Lillo Lagoons Loop, a moderate 26.0 miles (41.8 km) path. This trail leads through unique salt pans and bird-rich lagoons. Local mountain bikers also love the Camino Norte de la Laguna Loop, a 14.0 miles (22.6 km) trail leading through natural terrain, often completed in about 1 hour 50 minutes. Mountain biking around El Romeral is defined by historic windmills, extensive lagoons, and salt pans. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult ability levels. The routes in El Romeral are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.9 stars from more than 50 reviews. More than 100 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore El Romeral's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available around El Romeral?
A: There are over 20 mountain bike trails around El Romeral, offering a variety of experiences for different skill levels. The region is particularly known for its routes through scenic landscapes featuring historic windmills and unique lagoons.

Q: What are the difficulty levels of mountain bike trails in El Romeral?
A: The trails around El Romeral cater to various skill levels. You'll find one easy route, 20 moderate routes, and one challenging route. This variety ensures there's something suitable whether you're looking for a relaxed ride or a more demanding adventure.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes in El Romeral?
A: Yes, many of the mountain bike routes in El Romeral are circular. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1025994940/camino-norte-de-la-laguna-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Camino Norte de la Laguna Loop&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 22.6 km trail that takes about 1 hour 50 minutes to complete. Another option is the moderate &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1025989869/el-romeral-and-lillo-lagoons-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Romeral and Lillo Lagoons Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which is 41.8 km long and explores unique salt pans and bird-rich lagoons.

Q: What kind of landscapes can I expect to see on the mountain bike trails?
A: The mountain bike trails around El Romeral offer diverse landscapes. In Castilla-La Mancha, you'll encounter iconic windmills perched on hills, reminiscent of Don Quixote's era, and unique salt pans and bird-rich lagoons. The terrain often includes a mix of natural paths, unpaved roads, and some paved sections.

Q: Are there any family-friendly mountain bike trails in El Romeral?
A: While most trails are moderate, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1025994940/camino-norte-de-la-laguna-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Camino Norte de la Laguna Loop&lt;/a&gt; is classified as easy, making it a good option for families. It's a 22.6 km trail through natural terrain, generally completed in under two hours.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the mountain bike trails in El Romeral?
A: Yes, some areas of El Romeral are dog-friendly. For instance, the Multi Trail in Guadalajara (Alcarria - Guadalajara) explicitly allows dogs. When planning your ride, it's always a good idea to check specific trail regulations for the exact route you intend to take.

Q: What are some interesting landmarks or attractions near the mountain bike routes?
A: Along and near the mountain bike routes, you can explore several historical and cultural sites. Notable attractions include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6313232" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Church of Nuestra Señora de la Asunción (Tembleque)&lt;/a&gt;, the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5445505" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Plaza Mayor of Tembleque&lt;/a&gt;, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6269418" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Casa de las Torres&lt;/a&gt;. These offer a glimpse into the region's rich heritage.

Q: What do other mountain bikers say about the trails in El Romeral?
A: The mountain bike trails in El Romeral are highly regarded by the komoot community, with an average rating of 4.9 stars from nearly 60 reviews. Riders frequently praise the diverse scenery, particularly the iconic windmills and the serene lagoon areas, as well as the variety of terrain suitable for different skill levels.

Q: Is there parking available at the trailheads?
A: While specific parking details for each trailhead are not universally listed, areas like Tembleque, which serves as a starting point for routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1025851883/tembleque-and-el-romeral-windmills-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tembleque and El Romeral Windmills Trail&lt;/a&gt;, typically offer parking facilities within or near the town to accommodate visitors.

Q: What is the best time of year for mountain biking in El Romeral?
A: The best time for mountain biking in El Romeral is generally during the spring and autumn months. The weather is milder, making for more comfortable riding conditions. Summers can be very hot, and winters can be chilly, so plan accordingly and check local forecasts.

Q: Are there any long-distance mountain bike routes in the area?
A: Yes, if you're looking for a longer ride, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31870249" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Windmills of El Romeral – Palacio de las Torres loop from El Romeral&lt;/a&gt; is a challenging 93.3 km route. It offers an extensive exploration of the region's landscapes and historical sites, typically taking over 5 hours to complete.

Q: Can I find places to eat or stay near the mountain bike trails?
A: Towns like El Romeral and nearby Tembleque offer amenities such as cafes, restaurants, and accommodation options. These can serve as convenient bases for your mountain biking adventures, allowing you to refuel and rest after your rides.
tips: This road can be considered temporary, particularly in the lagoon sector since it depends on the level of its waters to be able to cross.

The Longar lagoon is part of the largest and most extensive saline lagoon complex in Spain with more than 400 hectares distributed in 2 lagoons.
It is a favorite place for bird watching and it is worth doing it to see the sunset that is usually worthy of a postcard.
A very important heritage element in El Romeral are its four windmills (Muela, Gorrinos, Crítica and Pechuga).

faqs.question: Is this mountain bike route suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: This route is rated as moderate for mountain biking. While it doesn't present extreme technical challenges, it's best suited for riders with some experience and a reasonable fitness level. The terrain includes varied surfaces, and you should be comfortable with some ascents and descents.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the route?
faqs.answer: The terrain is generally varied, as expected for a moderate mountain bike route. You'll encounter a mix of surfaces, likely including gravel and dirt paths. While specific technical details aren't extensively provided, the moderate rating suggests it's manageable without advanced skills, but be prepared for some changes in surface and elevation.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the route?
faqs.answer: The route offers several notable features. You'll pass by the unique Carril de la Albardiosa, the serene Laguna del Longar, and the historical El Romeral Windmills. The lagoons themselves, with their distinctive natural habitat and salt pans, are a major highlight.

faqs.question: What kind of wildlife can I expect to see?
faqs.answer: The area, particularly around the Albardiosa Lagoon, is known as a haven for various animal species, especially birds. It's an excellent spot for birdwatching, so keep an eye out for diverse birdlife that thrives in this unique natural habitat, which includes salt pans near a river.

faqs.question: When is the best time of year to mountain bike this route?
faqs.answer: Given the natural habitat and potential for heat, spring and autumn are generally the best times to mountain bike this route. The weather is usually milder, making for a more comfortable ride and better conditions for observing wildlife. Summers can be very hot in this region.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the trail?
faqs.answer: The route is a circular one starting from El Romeral. You can typically find parking within or near the town of El Romeral to begin your ride. Specific parking areas are usually well-marked or can be found near the main access points to the trail network.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay an entrance fee for this area?
faqs.answer: This trail is located within the Reserva Natural Lagunas de El Longar, Altillo Grande y Altillo Chica. Generally, no specific permits are required for mountain biking on designated trails within natural reserves, nor are there typically entrance fees for public access. However, always respect local regulations and signage regarding protected areas.

faqs.question: Is the Lagunas de la Albardiosa y del Longar mountain bike route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While many natural areas in Spain are dog-friendly, it's crucial to keep your dog on a leash, especially in a natural reserve like this one, which is a haven for birds and other wildlife. Always ensure your dog is under control and that you clean up after them to protect the delicate ecosystem.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route utilizes sections of several official trails. You'll find yourself on parts of the Colada de La Mancha, Carril de la Albardiosa, and Camino del Romeral, among others. This allows for a varied riding experience through the local network of paths.

faqs.question: What should I bring for a mountain bike ride on this route?
faqs.answer: For a moderate mountain bike route of this length (~41.8 km), you should bring plenty of water, snacks, a repair kit for your bike, a helmet, and appropriate clothing for the weather conditions. Sun protection is also highly recommended, especially during warmer months, as shade might be limited in some areas.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities like cafes or restaurants near the trail?
faqs.answer: Since the route starts and ends in El Romeral, you'll find cafes, restaurants, and other amenities within the town itself. It's a good idea to plan your refreshments before or after your ride, as there are unlikely to be facilities directly on the trail within the natural reserve.
